title: RadialMarkingMenu Object
description: Represents the marking menu which is the round menu that's displayed when the user right-clicks within Fusion. This supports customizing the contents of the marking menu.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: clear
methods.description: Completely clears the contents of the marking menu. If left in this state, the marking menu will not be displayed.

methods.name: create
methods.description: This is used to create a sub-menu in a marking menu. This method creates a new, empty marking menu which can then be assigned to a position in the displayed marking menu to define the sub-menu.

properties.name: eastCommand
properties.description: Gets and sets the command definition that's displayed in the East position (right) of the marking menu. Setting this to null indicates that the East position should be empty.   This can also return or be set with a MarkingMenu object which is used to have a sub-menu. New marking menus can be created using the create method and then assigned to the desired position in the marking menu.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: northCommand
properties.description: Gets and sets the command definition that's displayed in the North position (top) of the marking menu. Setting this to null indicates that the North position should be empty.   This can also return or be set with a MarkingMenu object which is used to have a sub-menu. New marking menus can be created using the create method and then assigned to the desired position in the marking menu.

properties.name: northeastCommand
properties.description: Gets and sets the command definition that's displayed in the Northeast position (top-right) of the marking menu. Setting this to null indicates that the Northeast position should be empty.   This can also return or be set with a MarkingMenu object which is used to have a sub-menu. New marking menus can be created using the create method and then assigned to the desired position in the marking menu.

properties.name: northwestCommand
properties.description: Gets and sets the command definition that's displayed in the Northwest position (upper-left) of the marking menu. Setting this to null indicates that the Northwest position should be empty.   This can also return or be set with a MarkingMenu object which is used to have a sub-menu. New marking menus can be created using the create method and then assigned to the desired position in the marking menu.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: southCommand
properties.description: Gets and sets the command definition that's displayed in the South position (bottom) of the marking menu. Setting this to null indicates that the South position should be empty.   This can also return or be set with a MarkingMenu object which is used to have a sub-menu. New marking menus can be created using the create method and then assigned to the desired position in the marking menu.

properties.name: southeastCommand
properties.description: Gets and sets the command definition that's displayed in the Southeast position (bottom-right) of the marking menu. Setting this to null indicates that the Southeast position should be empty.   This can also return or be set with a MarkingMenu object which is used to have a sub-menu. New marking menus can be created using the create method and then assigned to the desired position in the marking menu.

properties.name: southwestCommand
properties.description: Gets and sets the command definition that's displayed in the Southwest position (bottom-left) of the marking menu. Setting this to null indicates that the Southwest position should be empty.   This can also return or be set with a MarkingMenu object which is used to have a sub-menu. New marking menus can be created using the create method and then assigned to the desired position in the marking menu.

properties.name: text
properties.description: Gets and sets the text that is displayed in the parent marking menu to access a sub marking menu. This property is not used for the main marking menu and will return an empty string and setting it will have no effect.

properties.name: westCommand
properties.description: Gets and sets the command definition that's displayed in the West position (left) of the marking menu. Setting this to null indicates that the West position should be empty.   This can also return or be set with a MarkingMenu object which is used to have a sub-menu. New marking menus can be created using the create method and then assigned to the desired position in the marking menu.

classType.title: RadialMarkingMenu.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

clear.title: RadialMarkingMenu.clear Method
clear.description: Completely clears the contents of the marking menu. If left in this state, the marking menu will not be displayed.
clear.return_description: Returns true if the clear was successful.

related_samples.name: Marking Menu API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates how to customize marking menu and context menu. This sample is an add-in. To use it, create a new add-in using the "Scrips and Add-Ins" command. Use any name you would like for the add-in. In the folder where the add-in was created edit the add-in name.py file and replace it's entire contents with the sample code below. You can also delete all the other files that were created for the add-in except for add-in name.manifiest. Start the add-in from the "Scripts and Add-Ins" dialog. Now, with the add-in running, whenever you right-click in the Fusion window, you'll get an entirely customized context menu. The default marking menu has been modified by the add-in by removing the existing commands and adding some custom commands.

create.title: RadialMarkingMenu.create Method
create.description: This is used to create a sub-menu in a marking menu. This method creates a new, empty marking menu which can then be assigned to a position in the displayed marking menu to define the sub-menu.
create.return_description: Returns the newly created marking menu or null in the case of a failure.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The text that will be displayed in the parent menu to access this menu.

eastCommand.title: RadialMarkingMenu.eastCommand Property
eastCommand.description: Gets and sets the command definition that's displayed in the East position (right) of the marking menu. Setting this to null indicates that the East position should be empty. New marking menus can be created using the create method and then assigned to the desired position in the marking menu.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = radialMarkingMenu_var.eastCommand

# Set the value of the property.
radialMarkingMenu_var.eastCommand = propertyValue
